groups.name: flowable.test.diagnostics
groups.description: BPMN test-failure diagnostics: enriches a @FlowableProcessTest failure with a snapshot of the failing process instance.

properties.name: flowable.test.diagnostics.enabled
properties.description: Whether the BPMN failure-diagnostics capability is active at all.

properties.name: flowable.test.diagnostics.max-tracked-process-instances
properties.description: How many process instances a single test failure will run full diagnostics queries against; instances started beyond this cap are omitted, with a count of how many.

properties.name: flowable.test.diagnostics.max-activity-trail-entries
properties.description: How many entries of a process instance's activity trail a diagnostics snapshot includes.

properties.name: flowable.test.diagnostics.max-variable-value-length
properties.description: How many characters of a single process variable's rendered value a snapshot includes.

properties.name: flowable.test.diagnostics.include-failed-jobs
properties.description: Whether a diagnostics snapshot includes dead-letter job failures.

properties.name: flowable.test.diagnostics.redacted-variable-names
properties.description: Process variable names (case-insensitive substring match) rendered as [REDACTED] rather than dumped verbatim into a diagnostics snapshot.

properties.name: flowable.test.datasource.provider
properties.description: Which DataSource backs the embedded engine. 'auto' prefers embedded-postgres when io.zonky.test:embedded-postgres is on the test classpath, otherwise H2.

properties.name: flowable.test.datasource.embedded-postgres.instance-scope
properties.description: 'per-context' forks a fresh native embedded-postgres process per Spring context; 'shared' starts at most one process per JVM and provisions a fresh logical database per context on top of it.

properties.name: flowable.test.kafka.enabled
properties.description: Whether the embedded Kafka broker capability is active at all.

properties.name: flowable.test.kafka.channel-location
properties.description: Classpath pattern scanned for Flowable Kafka Event Registry *.channel descriptors when auto-discovering topics for the embedded broker.

properties.name: flowable.test.kafka.partitions
properties.description: Partition count for every auto-discovered and additional Kafka topic created on the embedded broker.

properties.name: flowable.test.kafka.broker-scope
properties.description: 'shared' reuses one JVM-wide embedded Kafka broker singleton across every Spring context; 'per-context' starts a fresh broker for each context.

properties.name: flowable.test.http-mocks.enabled
properties.description: Whether the declarative WireMock HTTP stubbing capability is active at all.

properties.name: flowable.test.http-mocks.root
properties.description: Classpath root scanned for one WireMock mapping folder per immediate subfolder ('<root>/<service-name>/mappings/*.json').

properties.name: flowable.test.http-mocks.services
properties.description: Explicit, declared list of HTTP mock service names to start, replacing the default classpath scan of every immediate subfolder under the configured root. A declared name with no matching mappings folder fails fast, before the ApplicationContext refreshes.

properties.name: flowable.test.processes.root
properties.description: Classpath root BPMN process file names resolve against, both for the default deployment allow-list and for @FlowableProcessTest(processes = ...) / ProcessTestHarness#deployProcess.

properties.name: flowable.test.processes.deploy
properties.description: Explicit, declared list of BPMN process file names (under flowable.test.processes.root) to deploy by default, replacing Flowable's own classpath scan. Absent, Flowable's own scan deploys every process file it finds, unchanged.

values.description: Prefer embedded-postgres when it's on the test classpath, otherwise H2.
Force H2 even if embedded-postgres is on the classpath.
Fork a fresh native embedded-postgres process per Spring context (default).
Share one JVM-wide embedded-postgres process across contexts, each with its own logical database.
Reuse one JVM-wide embedded Kafka broker singleton across every Spring context (default).
Start a fresh embedded Kafka broker for each Spring context.
